source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that informed or directionally support each lever's savings range. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.

condition_precedents: Branch-level P&L and footfall data available for ≥ 24 months
RBI approval for branch closure obtained per banking regulation
Digital channel migration capacity (mobile / internet banking) confirmed
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)

execution_playbook.step: Map 24-month branch P&L, footfall, and digital-adoption by branch; rank viability against break-even threshold
Cluster branches into full-service, lite-format, and digital-only; model revenue and cost impact per scenario
Initiate RBI closure approvals; negotiate lease exits; redeploy ATMs/BCs to adjacent catchments
Execute digital migration campaign for displaced customers; track balances and transaction shift to digital channels
Monitor revenue retention, occupancy cost savings, and customer NPS post-format change; rebaseline quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: FACILITIES spend >3% of revenue with ≥20% of branches showing negative unit P&L for ≥2 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Over-networked footprint with structural cost drag

diagnostic_signals.signal: Digital channel adoption >60% in clusters with overlapping full-service branches within 2 km
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Format rationalisation feasible without material revenue loss

diagnostic_signals.signal: Fixed occupancy cost >40% of total branch OPEX in bottom-quartile branches
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Cost structure savings achievable via format or closure

required_data_fields: Branch-level P&L for ≥24 months (revenue, direct cost, allocated overhead)
Monthly footfall and transaction count per branch
Digital adoption by geography (mobile/internet banking penetration)
Lease terms, WALE, and break-clause positions per branch
RBI branch closure approval requirements and timelines

condition_precedents: Process mining analysis completed for high-volume back-office processes
Core banking system APIs exposed for workflow automation
RBI operational risk and cyber security review of automation completed

execution_playbook.step: Run process mining on top-10 back-office processes by volume (NEFT/RTGS, account opening, trade-finance docs) to identify STP candidates
Expose CBS APIs for workflow automation; design Front-to-Back STP architecture and data-flow diagram
Deploy RPA bots and API integrations in waves—Wave 1 high-volume/low-risk, Wave 2 regulatory processes, Wave 3 exception handling
Complete RBI operational-risk and cyber-security review; obtain IS Audit sign-off before production cutover
Stabilise bots, hand over to run team, and track FTE release and error-rate reduction monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Manual touchpoints >40% in high-volume back-office processes (account opening, reconciliation, trade-finance docs)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: STP automation ROI is achievable

diagnostic_signals.signal: Back-office headcount cost as % of OPEX above peer benchmark (OUTSOURCED/IT >8% revenue)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digitisation opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Error or rework rate in back-office processes >5% of transaction volume
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Quality and cost-reduction case for automation is compelling

required_data_fields: Process inventory with transaction volumes and manual vs automated step breakdown
CBS API capability documentation and integration readiness
FTE count and loaded labour cost per back-office process
IT application landscape map
RBI IT-framework compliance checklist

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: IT Vendor & Core Banking Platform Consolidation
condition_precedents: IT application landscape rationalisation roadmap approved by CTO
Core banking system upgrade / migration plan approved and budgeted
RBI IT framework compliance assessment completed for new systems

execution_playbook.step: Complete IT application-landscape audit: map all active applications to vendor, annual cost, and usage metrics
Build rationalization roadmap—classify apps as keep/consolidate/retire; develop CBS upgrade/migration plan
Run competitive RFPs for consolidated IT categories (infrastructure, middleware, testing); negotiate multi-year terms
Execute CBS migration in controlled phases with RBI IT-framework compliance assessment at each stage gate
Decommission retired applications; track licence spend reduction and vendor-count trend monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: IT vendor count >50 with top-10 vendors representing <60% of IT spend
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Fragmented supply base with consolidation potential

diagnostic_signals.signal: IT spend >5% of revenue with >30% on maintenance of legacy or duplicate systems
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Material cost reduction available through rationalization

diagnostic_signals.signal: Duplicate capability across ≥2 applications in same functional domain (multiple CRMs, reporting tools)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Platform consolidation case is justified

required_data_fields: Full IT application inventory with vendor, annual cost, and active user count
CBS vendor agreement terms and migration risk assessment
IT spend by sub-category (infrastructure, applications, services, telecom)
RBI IT framework compliance status per system
Contract terms and renewal dates for top-20 IT vendors

condition_precedents: V-KYC / digital KYC approved under RBI Master Directions
AI-based AML transaction monitoring system validated
PMLA compliance review of automated alert disposition completed
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)

execution_playbook.step: Map end-to-end KYC (onboarding + periodic refresh) and AML (transaction monitoring + alert disposition) workflows; measure FTE-hours per case
Select V-KYC platform and AI AML engine; validate against RBI Master Directions and PMLA requirements
Integrate V-KYC with CBS and document management; run AI AML model in shadow mode for parallel validation
Complete PMLA compliance review of automated alert disposition; obtain internal-audit and external-auditor sign-off
Go live; track KYC turnaround, AML false-positive rate, and FTE release monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: KYC/AML operations headcount or outsourced cost >15% of total compliance budget
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scale of manual effort justifies automation investment

diagnostic_signals.signal: AML alert false-positive rate >85% consuming analyst time without commensurate risk reduction
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI-tuned model will materially reduce alert volume and cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average KYC turnaround time >5 days (individual) or >15 days (corporate)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: STP opportunity exists with material customer-experience upside

required_data_fields: KYC workflow map with FTE-hours per step and monthly case volumes
AML alert data: total volume, disposition rate, false-positive rate, and analyst hours
CBS API readiness and V-KYC regulatory approval status
PMLA/RBI Master Directions compliance checklist
Current KYC/AML vendor contracts and SLA performance data

condition_precedents: Predictive dialler and AI-based collections scoring deployed
Collections agency panel rationalised (performance-based empanelment)
IBC / SARFAESI process automation for secured assets implemented
industry_sources: INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Segment NPA portfolio by bucket (0–30, 31–60, 61–90, 90+ DPD), product, and geography; identify highest-yield recovery segments
Deploy predictive collections scoring and AI call-routing; rationalise agency panel to performance-based empanelment
Automate IBC/SARFAESI process flows for secured recovery; integrate legal case management with collections workflow
Launch digital self-cure channels (WhatsApp, net-banking EMI) for early-bucket accounts to reduce outbound cost
Track recovery rate by segment, cost-per-recovery, and agent productivity; rebalance channel mix quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Collections cost as % of NPA book >2% indicating a manual-heavy model
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation and agency rationalisation will reduce cost per recovery

diagnostic_signals.signal: Collections agency panel >10 vendors with bottom-quartile agencies contributing <15% of recoveries
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Performance-based empanelment will improve recovery yield

diagnostic_signals.signal: Digital self-cure adoption <20% for early-bucket (0–30 DPD) accounts
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Shift to digital channels will reduce outbound call cost

required_data_fields: NPA portfolio by bucket, product, and geography (12-month history)
Collections agency performance data (recovery rate, cost per recovery, case allocation)
IBC/SARFAESI case inventory and resolution timelines
Digital channel adoption rates by customer segment
Collections FTE count and cost breakdown by activity

condition_precedents: Treasury management system (Murex / Kondor) upgraded for automation
ALM framework reviewed by board and RBI-appointed actuary / auditor
Correspondent banking relationships rationalised
industry_sources: Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct ALM gap analysis: map funding cost, liquidity buffers, and correspondent relationships; benchmark against peer banks
Upgrade TMS (Murex/Kondor) to automate deal capture, limit monitoring, and regulatory return generation
Rationalise correspondent banking panel—retain key corridors, exit low-volume relationships
Automate intra-day liquidity monitoring and SLR/CRR reporting; reduce manual FTE overhead
Track ALM cost, correspondent-fee reduction, and treasury-ops FTE productivity quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Treasury operations team >15 FTEs with >50% of time on manual reconciliation and regulatory reporting
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will materially reduce treasury ops cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Correspondent banking fee spend >₹2 Cr/year with >20 active correspondents
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Panel rationalisation opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: SLR/CRR returns prepared manually by >2 FTEs taking >3 days per period
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation of regulatory returns is justified

required_data_fields: ALM gap report and liquidity buffer composition
Correspondent banking fee schedule and transaction volumes by corridor
Treasury ops FTE count and time allocation by activity
Current TMS capability assessment and upgrade roadmap
SLR/CRR and RBI regulatory reporting FTE cost and frequency

condition_precedents: Historical transaction fraud data labelled (≥ 2 years, min 10,000 confirmed fraud cases)
ML fraud scoring engine integrated with core banking / payment switch
RBI cyber security framework compliance for AI fraud models confirmed

execution_playbook.step: Label historical transaction fraud data (≥2 years, min 10,000 confirmed fraud cases); build feature-engineering pipeline
Develop and validate ML fraud scoring engine vs current rules engine using holdout dataset; document precision/recall tradeoffs
Integrate scoring engine with CBS/payment switch in real-time; complete RBI cyber-security framework compliance review
Run 60-day parallel validation: compare fraud catch rate and false-positive rate vs legacy rules engine
Decommission legacy rules engine; monitor fraud loss rate, detection rate, and model drift monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Fraud loss as % of disbursements >0.1% indicating inadequacy of current detection
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ML model will improve catch rate and reduce fraud losses

diagnostic_signals.signal: False-positive rate on current rules engine >60% generating customer friction and analyst overhead
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ML will reduce false positives and investigation cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Manual fraud investigation team >20 FTEs with case backlog >500 outstanding
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation and better triage will reduce investigation cost

required_data_fields: Transaction history ≥2 years with confirmed fraud labels
Current fraud rules engine architecture and detection-rate data
CBS/payment-switch API capability for real-time ML scoring integration
Fraud loss data by product, channel, and customer segment
RBI cyber-security framework compliance checklist

condition_precedents: Regulatory data dictionary aligned with RBI XBRL / XSLT taxonomy
Single source of truth for financial data (data warehouse / data lake) established
Parallel run validation completed before switching off manual returns
industry_sources: Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)

execution_playbook.step: Inventory all RBI/SEBI regulatory returns: frequency, data sources, FTE-hours, and manual touchpoints per return
Align financial data dictionary with RBI XBRL/XSLT taxonomy; establish single source of truth in data warehouse
Build automated generation for top-10 high-effort returns (SLR, CRR, CRILC, large exposure); configure validation rules
Run two full reporting cycles in parallel (automated vs manual); reconcile and sign off with internal audit
Decommission manual returns; track FTE release and on-time submission rate

diagnostic_signals.signal: Regulatory reporting team >10 FTEs spending >60% of time on data extraction and manual report preparation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will release significant FTE capacity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Number of unique regulatory returns >30 with >5 source systems feeding each return
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Data integration and automation investment is justified

diagnostic_signals.signal: Late or restated returns ≥2 per year due to manual data aggregation errors
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Quality and compliance-risk reduction from automation is material

required_data_fields: Inventory of all regulatory returns (name, regulator, frequency, source systems, FTE-hours)
RBI XBRL/XSLT taxonomy mapping to chart of accounts
Data warehouse/lake architecture and data-quality assessment
Compliance team FTE count and time allocation per return
Historical late/restated return log with root-cause analysis
